title: The One With The Tiny T-shirt

expressions.dialogue: I've kinda of had this ah, this **crush** on you.
expressions.speaker: Mark
expressions.nuance: 'like'보다 감정 강도가 세고 'love'보다 가벼운 두근거림을 뜻한다. 1990년대부터 현재까지 일상적으로 쓰이는 표현이다.
expressions.example: I think I have a crush on my new coworker. → 나 새로 온 동료한테 설레는 것 같아.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Well, CHP because I used to have a crush on Eric Estrada. And ZXY becuase I think it sounds zexy.

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Ugh, okay, I have an enormous crush on you. But because you're a client, I can't ask you out, even though you give me y'know, the feeling.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Rach, if you have a crush on this guy, why would you hire him? I mean y'know you can't date him right?

expressions.dialogue: I'd really like to **ask you out** sometime.
expressions.speaker: Mark
expressions.nuance: 'Do you want to go out with me?'보다 자연스럽고 간결하다. 현재도 동일하게 사용되는 필수 표현이다.
expressions.example: He finally asked her out after months of hesitation. → 그는 몇 달을 망설이다 드디어 그녀에게 데이트를 신청했다.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: He finally asked you out?

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Priesthood! Look Ross, I'm telling you, she has no idea what you're thinking. If you don't ask her out soon you're going to end up stuck in the zone forever.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: You think it would be okay if I asked out your sister?

expressions.expression: I was wondering if you'd like to ~
expressions.dialogue: (In his head) **I was wondering if you'd like to** go to a movie with me sometime.
expressions.speaker: Gunther
expressions.nuance: 'Do you want to ~?'보다 훨씬 부드럽고 정중한 요청 표현이다. 데이트 신청이나 공손한 부탁에 자주 쓰이며 격식·비격식 상황 모두 사용 가능하다.
expressions.example: I was wondering if you'd like to join us for dinner tonight. → 오늘 저녁 식사 같이 하실 수 있을까 해서요.

occ.speaker: Mr. Waltham
occ.text: I-I was wondering, my niece you see is in from London-well Shropshire really but y'know-well she's about your age I say. Anyway I have tickets for the opera, Die Fledermaus, and I was wondering if you'd like to keep her company this evening?

occ.speaker: Cop
occ.text: Okay, so since umm, you're not going to jail tonight I was wondering if you would like to go to dinner with me?

expressions.dialogue: (**In his head**) Say Rachel, I was wondering if you'd like to go to a movie with me sometime.
expressions.speaker: Gunther
expressions.meaning: 속으로, 마음속에서 (실제로 말하지 않고 생각만 하는 상태)
expressions.nuance: 'in my head'는 '머릿속으로만 생각한 것'을 뜻한다. 상상이나 내적 독백을 묘사할 때 쓰며, 실제 발화와 구분하는 표현이다.
expressions.example: I had the whole conversation planned in my head, but when I saw her I couldn't say a word. → 대화를 머릿속으로 다 계획해뒀는데, 막상 그녀를 보니 한마디도 못 했어.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Well, I was kinda supposed to be headed for Aruba on my honeymoon, so nothing!

occ.speaker: Mr. Tribbiani
occ.text: No, it's only been six years. I just wanted to put a nice memory in your head so you'd know that I wasn't always such a terrible guy. ...Joe. Y'ever been in love?

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: You know, Rachel, when you ran out of your wedding, I was there for you. I put a roof over your head, and if that means nothing to you... (Rachel isn't buying it, desperate) twenty dollars an hour.

expressions.dialogue: voice recognition is gonna be **pretty much** standard on any computer you buy
expressions.speaker: Pete
expressions.nuance: 100%는 아니지만 거의 그렇다는 의미의 구어체 부사로, 일상 대화에서 매우 자주 등장한다. 현재도 동일하게 쓰인다.
expressions.example: I'm pretty much done with the project. Just need to review it once more. → 프로젝트 거의 다 끝났어. 한 번만 더 검토하면 돼.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Yeah, I think for us, kissing is pretty much like an opening act, y'know? I mean it's like the stand-up comedian you have to sit through before Pink Floyd comes out.

occ.speaker: Pizza Guy
occ.text: No, pretty much just a towel.

occ.speaker: Bob
occ.text: Yep. Pretty much.

expressions.dialogue: I mean it's **driving me crazy**.
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.meaning: ~가 나를 미치게 한다, 너무 답답하거나 짜증스럽다
expressions.nuance: 화가 나거나 이해할 수 없는 상황을 강조하는 과장 표현이다. 부정적 감정 외에 '헷갈려 죽겠다'는 뉘앙스로도 쓴다.
expressions.example: This bug is driving me crazy. I've been on it for two hours. → 이 버그 때문에 미치겠어. 두 시간째 붙잡고 있어.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Now I'm mommy in this little play? Alright look, I refuse to get sucked into this like, weird little Geller dimension thing OK. So I'm gonna go and take a nice long hot bubble bath because you kids are driving me crazy. [goes in the bathroom]

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Ooh, that girl that I hate, eww, drives me crazy, eww, eww, oh!

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Oh, we used to drive each other crazy playing the shadow game.

expressions.dialogue: I'm **not attracted to him** at all!!
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.nuance: 외모뿐 아니라 감정적 끌림 전반을 포괄하는 표현이다. 'at all'을 붙이면 '전혀'라는 강조가 더해진다.
expressions.example: He's a great person, but I'm just not attracted to him at all. → 좋은 사람인데, 그냥 전혀 끌리질 않아.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: And you're still not attracted to him at all?

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Is that why he's acting so weird...? He's jealous...? Oh my God, that is crazy. It's not like I'm attracted to Geoffrey...

expressions.dialogue: She's so brilliantly incisive **when it comes to** deconstructing the psyche of the American middle class.
expressions.speaker: Kate
expressions.nuance: 특정 주제·분야로 화제를 좁힐 때 쓰는 연결 표현이다. 격식·비격식 상황 모두에서 사용 가능하다.
expressions.example: When it comes to cooking Korean food, nobody beats my mom. → 한식 요리에 있어서는 우리 엄마를 당할 사람이 없어.

occ.speaker: Alice
occ.text: Oh no, but when it comes to love, what does age matter?

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: When it comes down to it, you would risk your life for Ross before you would for me. That's the bottom line.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: No. When it comes to sweets, he's surprisingly strict.

expressions.dialogue: **Forget about it.** She rocks!
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 말할 것도 없지, 두말하면 잔소리지 (= 당연히 최고야)
expressions.nuance: 이탈리아계 뉴욕 영어에서 온 표현으로, 맥락에 따라 '당연하지' 또는 '말도 안 돼'의 뜻으로 쓰인다. 현재도 쓰이지만 뉴욕 색깔이 강한 느낌이다.
expressions.example: Best ramen in the city? Forget about it. → 이 도시 최고의 라멘? 말할 것도 없지.

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: (as Ursula): Yeah, um... (nervously clears her throat) You know you, you should just forget about what I said under the bridge, I was talkin' crazy that night, I was so drunk!

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Forget about it.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Oh, you're right I, I should play in the hay. Forget about the fact that I just dropped 400 dollars to replace a bracelet that I hated to begin with. Bring on the hay. [sits down at the bar]

expressions.dialogue: Forget about it. She **rocks**!
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.nuance: 1990년대 미국 청소년 문화에서 유행한 슬랭이다. 현재도 구어에서 쓰이지만 약간 레트로한 느낌이 있다.
expressions.example: This band rocks! I can't believe I never heard them before. → 이 밴드 진짜 최고야! 지금까지 어떻게 몰랐지.

occ.speaker: Janice
occ.text: Well, I knew you had the Rockys, and so I figured, you know, you can wear Bullwinkle and Bullwinkle, or you can wear Rocky and Rocky,or, you can mix and match, moose and squirrel. Whatever you want.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Okaaay, (Gestures) rock, hard place, me.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: When I saw him get off that plane with her, I really thought I hit rock bottom. But today, it's like there's rock bottom, 50 feet of crap, then me.

expressions.dialogue: like I'd be **cheating on Ross** or something
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.nuance: 연인 관계에서의 불성실한 행동을 뜻하는 필수 표현이다. 문장 끝의 'or something'은 확신이 없거나 표현을 부드럽게 할 때 붙인다.
expressions.example: She felt guilty even though she'd done nothing wrong — like she was cheating on him. → 아무 잘못도 안 했는데도 그에게 바람 피우는 것 같은 죄책감을 느꼈다.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Come on, you're going to Bloomingdale's with Julie? That's like cheating on Rachel in her house of worship.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Yeah, never cheat on Rachel.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Yeah, maybe he just cheated on you.

expressions.dialogue: Oh, well, I guess I **had that one coming**.
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.meaning: 자업자득이다, 당해도 싸다, 마땅히 받아야 할 결과다
expressions.nuance: 자신이 한 행동의 결과로 나쁜 일이 일어났을 때 인정하는 표현이다. 'You had it coming' 형태로 상대에게 쓰기도 한다.
expressions.example: He got fired for showing up late every day. He had it coming. → 매일 지각하다 해고됐어. 자업자득이지.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Mom says it's all of Manhattan, parts of Brooklyn and Queens, and they have no idea when it's coming back on.

occ.speaker: Richard
occ.text: No. You have got it completely wrong. John Savage was deerhunter, no legs, John Voit was coming home, couldn't feel his legs.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: 'Cause I was going by it the other day and I saw that there was a stock with my initials, MEG, on it and, well, sometimes I have to watch for two or three hours before it comes up again but when it does, it's pretty exciting.

expressions.dialogue: maybe the crazy **fog has lifted** and she realises that life without me sucks.
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.meaning: 혼란스럽거나 감정에 휩쓸린 상태에서 벗어나다, 정신이 들다
expressions.nuance: 감정적 혼란·집착을 '안개(fog)'에 비유한 관용 표현이다. 구어보다 약간 문학적이며, 현재도 'the fog lifted' 형태로 쓰인다.
expressions.example: A month after the breakup, the fog finally lifted and I realized I was better off. → 헤어지고 한 달이 지나서야 안개가 걷히듯 내가 더 나아졌다는 걸 깨달았다.

expressions.dialogue: she realises that life without me.... a-**sucks**.
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.nuance: 1980년대부터 쓰이기 시작해 90년대에 폭발적으로 확산된 속어다. 현재 가장 자주 쓰이는 부정 표현 중 하나지만 격식적인 상황에서는 피해야 한다.
expressions.example: Traffic on Monday morning really sucks. → 월요일 아침 교통 상황은 진짜 최악이야.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Welcome to the real world! It sucks. You're gonna love it!

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Oh, that sucks!

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Heads up Ross! (Monica scores on Chandler and Joey) Score! (points at Chandler) You suck!

expressions.expression: Bullets have left guns slower (비교 과장법)
expressions.dialogue: You slept with somebody three hours after you thought you broke up. **Bullets have left guns slower!**
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 총알도 저보다는 느리게 나갔을 거야 — 놀라울 만큼 빨리 행동했음을 과장하는 표현
expressions.nuance: 어떤 일이 믿기 어려울 정도로 빠르게 일어났을 때 쓰는 비교 과장법(hyperbole)이다. 'X don't/doesn't happen slower than Y' 구조로 응용 가능하다.
expressions.example: He got a new girlfriend a week after the breakup. Bullets have left guns slower. → 헤어진 지 일주일 만에 새 여자친구가 생겼대. 총알도 저보다는 느리게 나갔겠다.

expressions.dialogue: She's **moving on**!
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.nuance: 이별 후 상대가 새 관계를 시작하거나 회복해나가는 것을 표현할 때 쓴다. 매우 일상적이고 현재도 동일하게 사용된다.
expressions.example: You need to move on. Dwelling on the past won't help. → 이제 앞으로 나아가야 해. 과거에 머물러 있어 봤자 도움이 안 돼.

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Well, he made a move on me.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: He's right, man. Please. Move on. Go to China. Eat Chinese food.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: I know, I know I was, but there was always this little voice inside that kept saying it's never gonna happen, move on. You know whose voice that was?

expressions.dialogue: I'm doing this **for the wrong reasons**, y'know?
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.nuance: 'for the right reasons'와 짝을 이루는 표현이다. 행동의 동기가 순수하지 않음을 스스로 인정할 때 쓴다.
expressions.example: I don't want to apologize just to end the argument — that would be doing it for the wrong reasons. → 싸움을 끝내려고 사과하고 싶진 않아. 그건 잘못된 이유로 하는 거잖아.

expressions.dialogue: **Fair, schmair!** Y'know?
expressions.speaker: Mark
expressions.meaning: '공평하다고? 그게 무슨 상관이야!' — 상대방의 말을 유머러스하게 일축하는 표현
expressions.nuance: 유대계 미국인 영어(Yiddish)에서 온 패턴으로, 앞 단어의 첫 음절을 'schm-'으로 바꿔 그 개념을 무력화한다. 현재는 다소 구식이거나 코믹한 느낌이다.
expressions.example: Rules, schmules! Just eat the cake. → 규칙이고 뭐고! 그냥 케이크나 먹어.

expressions.expression: in a place (in my life) where ~
expressions.dialogue: right now I'm just **in a place in my life** where I need to focus on me.
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.meaning: 지금 내 삶의 어느 지점에서, 지금 나는 ~한 상황에 있다
expressions.nuance: 현재의 감정 상태나 삶의 단계를 완곡하게 설명하는 표현이다. 주로 거절이나 변명을 부드럽게 전달할 때 등장한다.
expressions.example: I'm just not in a place where I can commit to a relationship right now. → 지금은 관계에 헌신할 수 있는 상황이 아니야.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: No, no. The vet says unless he's in a place where he has regular access to some... monkey lovin,' he's just gonna get vicious. I've just gotta get him into a zoo.

occ.speaker: Young Ethan
occ.text: I am telling you, up until I was, like nine, I thought that gunpoint was an actual place where crimes happen.

occ.speaker: Leslie
occ.text: I ran into Vlad at the place where they sell the big fish, and he said you played here a lot, so umm....

expressions.dialogue: you ah, **feel like getting** a cup of coffee?
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.nuance: 'want to'보다 훨씬 가볍고 즉흥적인 느낌이다. 제안이나 권유할 때 부담 없이 쓸 수 있는 표현이다.
expressions.example: Do you feel like grabbing lunch? → 점심이나 먹으러 갈까?

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Man, we gotta do something about that guy. This morning, I caught him looking into our apartment. It creeps me out! I feel like I can't do stuff!

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Because, I feel like I wouldn't just be going out with him. I would be going out with all of you. Oh, and there would just be all this pressure, and I don't wanna...

occ.speaker: Rach
occ.text: I don't know, I, I feel like I had a dream about you last night but I, I don't remember.

expressions.dialogue: I **don't want this to come out wrong**, but
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.nuance: 솔직하거나 민감한 말을 꺼내기 전에 오해를 미리 방지하는 완충 표현이다. 'I don't want this to come out wrong, but...' 형태로 자주 쓰인다.
expressions.example: I don't want this to come out wrong, but I think your plan needs more work. → 이상하게 들릴 수 있는데, 네 계획에 보완이 좀 더 필요할 것 같아.

expressions.dialogue: That's so Monica can **keep track**.
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.meaning: ~을 빠짐없이 파악하고 있다, 놓치지 않고 관리하다
expressions.nuance: 물건, 정보, 상황 등을 지속적으로 확인·관리하는 것을 표현한다. 활용 범위가 넓고 매우 일상적이다.
expressions.example: I use an app to keep track of my expenses. → 앱으로 지출을 관리해.

expressions.dialogue: Ohh, **you're out of your mind**.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.nuance: 'You're crazy'의 구어 표현이다. 상대방의 말이나 행동이 황당하거나 믿기 어려울 때 반응으로 쓴다.
expressions.example: You paid $500 for that? Are you out of your mind? → 그거 500달러 줬다고? 제정신이야?

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Are you out of your mind?

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: What? Are you out of your mind? Lets throw some jello on them.

expressions.dialogue: you have **nothing but** talk about her for the last 48 hours!
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.nuance: 특정 행동을 쉬지 않고 계속했음을 강조하는 표현이다. 'have done nothing but + 동사원형' 패턴으로 쓰인다.
expressions.example: She's done nothing but complain since she got here. → 그녀는 여기 온 이후로 불평만 늘어놓았어.

expressions.dialogue: you'd be **pulling her pigtails** and pushing her down now!
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 좋아하는 상대에게 일부러 짓궂게 굴다 (어린아이 같은 짝사랑 방식)
expressions.nuance: 어릴 때 좋아하는 여자애 머리카락을 당기는 행동에서 유래한 비유다. 성인에게 쓰면 '사실은 좋아서 그러는 거 아니야?'라는 뉘앙스가 담긴다.
expressions.example: Stop being mean to her all the time. You're basically pulling her pigtails. → 그렇게 맨날 짓궂게 굴지 마. 사실 좋아서 그러는 거잖아.
